sidewalk_stop.title: Public Space Encounter
sidewalk_stop.description: Standard interaction in a public walkway.

start.title: Initial Encounter
start.description: An officer approaches and asks for identification while you are walking.

choices.text: Provide ID and ask if you are free to go
choices.feedback: Good. Complying with basic ID requests safely establishes baseline cooperation while asserting your rights.

choices.text: Walk away abruptly
choices.feedback: Walking away from a lawful stop can result in detainment or escalation. Always ask if you are free to leave first.

victory.title: ENCOUNTER MANAGED
victory.description: You successfully navigated the situation by staying calm and knowing your rights.

firearm_stop.title: The High-Stakes Stop
firearm_stop.description: Advanced scenario involving a weapons investigation.

start.title: Initial Contact
start.description: A cruiser pulls onto the sidewalk, blocking your path. The officer shouts: 'Keep your hands where I can see them! Why are you walking so heavy on that right side?'

choices.text: Keep walking and ignore them.
choices.feedback: DANGEROUS. Ignoring a direct 'show of authority' in this context can justify a pursuit.

choices.text: Stop, keep hands visible, and ask: 'Officer, am I free to leave?'
choices.feedback: CORRECT. This establishes whether this is a consensual encounter or a seizure.

choices.text: Put your hands in your pockets.
choices.feedback: HIGH RISK. Reaching for pockets during a firearm stop escalates the situation immediately.

detainment_check.title: The Investigation
detainment_check.description: The officer gets out. 'We got a call about a weapon. We need to check you out for safety. You got anything on you?'

choices.text: Say: 'I'm not answering questions without a lawyer.'
choices.feedback: EXCELLENT. Invoking your 5th Amendment right prevents self-incrimination.

choices.text: Say: 'I don't have anything, I'm just a student.'
choices.feedback: CAUTION. Offering personal info gives them more 'reasonable suspicion' to hold you longer.

search_scenario.title: The Pat Down
search_scenario.description: The officer moves in: 'I'm gonna do a quick pat-down for my safety. Don't move.' He reaches for your waistline.

choices.text: State clearly: 'I do not consent to this search.'
choices.feedback: PERFECT. You protect your legal rights even if you cannot physically stop the frisk.

choices.text: Twist away to avoid the touch.
choices.feedback: DANGEROUS. Physical resistance gives them the right to use force.

victory.title: KNOWLEDGE IS POWER
victory.description: You successfully navigated a high-risk stop by remaining calm and withdrawing consent.

possession_class_a.title: The Observed 'Drop'
possession_class_a.description: A cruiser slows down and an officer steps out questioning an item on the ground.

start.title: Phase 1: The Observed 'Drop'
start.description: You're walking in Roxbury. A cruiser slows down and an officer steps out. 'Hey, hold up. I saw you drop something. What was that?'

choices.text: Panic and try to kick the item under a nearby car.
choices.feedback: DANGEROUS: BPD Rule 103 defines this as 'evasive behavior,' which provides probable cause for tampering.

choices.text: Pick it up and hand it to the officer to show you're 'clean'.
choices.feedback: TRAP: Under BPD Rule 310, physical control and handing over an item legally confirms 'possession.'

choices.text: Immediately say: 'I didn't drop anything. Am I free to go?'
choices.feedback: SMART: This triggers BPD Rule 323 logic. You are testing the legality of the detention.

the_frisk_limit.title: Phase 2: The Safety Frisk
the_frisk_limit.description: The officer says, 'I'm not so sure. I'm doing a quick pat-down for my safety to check for needles or weapons.'

choices.text: State clearly: 'I do not consent to this search or any search of my person.'
choices.feedback: EXCELLENT: Per BPD Rule 334, withholding consent ensures evidence found illegally may be suppressed.

choices.text: Say: 'Fine, but I don't have anything on me.'
choices.feedback: WAIVER: Giving 'voluntary consent' under Rule 334 waives your 4th Amendment protections.

victory.title: RIGHTS PROTECTED
victory.description: You used Rule 323 and Rule 334 to protect your legal standing.

assault_battery.title: Scene of Conflict
assault_battery.description: Navigating police arrival after a physical altercation.

start.title: Phase 1: The Scene
start.description: A heated argument turns physical. BPD arrives. 'Hands up! What happened here?'

choices.text: Explain that the other person started it.
choices.feedback: CAUTION: Under Rule 332, admitting to contact establishes the elements of a crime.

choices.text: Ask: 'Am I free to go, or am I being detained?'
choices.feedback: SMART: Forces the officer to articulate 'Reasonable Suspicion' per Rule 323.

the_medical_tactic.title: Phase 2: Investigatory Pressure
the_medical_tactic.description: Officer: 'If you tell me the truth now, it will look better for you later.'

choices.text: State: 'I am invoking my right to remain silent and I want a lawyer.'
choices.feedback: EXCELLENT: Per Rule 332, once you request counsel, all questioning must cease.

choices.text: Tell the officer exactly where they hit you.
choices.feedback: TRAP: Providing details creates a recorded admission of your involvement.

victory.title: LEGAL SHIELD ACTIVE
victory.description: You protected your future by refusing a street-level confession.

disorderly_conduct.title: Disorderly & Recording
disorderly_conduct.description: Handling dispersal orders and recording police activity.

start.title: Phase 1: Dispersal Order
start.description: Officer: 'You kids are making too much noise. Move along or I'm locking you up for Disorderly.'

choices.text: State: 'We will move, but I want to document we were peaceful.'
choices.feedback: SMART: Complying while asserting rights prevents arrest under Rule 202.

choices.text: Yell back that it is a public park.
choices.feedback: RISKY: Rule 103 allows citations for 'tumultuous' behavior.

the_camera_block.title: Phase 2: The Camera Block
the_camera_block.description: Officer: 'Put the phone away. You're interfering.'

choices.text: Step back and say: 'I have a 1st Amendment right to film in public.'
choices.feedback: EXCELLENT: Rule 405 protects filming as long as you don't 'physically interfere.'

choices.text: Push his hand away.
choices.feedback: DANGER: Physical contact is Assault on a P.O.

victory.title: KNOWLEDGE IS POWER
victory.description: You avoided a 'contempt of cop' arrest using Rule 103 and 405.
